danger_signals.visual_form: flaming/burning building sprite with darkened coloring
danger_signals.meaning: structure under attack or severely damaged, imminent destruction risk

danger_signals.visual_form: burnt ground scorch marks where building previously stood
danger_signals.meaning: area has been destroyed; enemy presence was here; loss of resource/defensive position

danger_signals.visual_form: darkened or damaged unit sprite appearance
danger_signals.meaning: unit is injured or weakened; reduced combat effectiveness

danger_signals.visual_form: red-colored units on screen approaching player settlements

safety_signals.visual_form: fully-colored, undamaged building sprite with standard appearance
safety_signals.meaning: structure is functional and safe; defensive capability active

safety_signals.visual_form: lumber and gold numbers displayed in top UI bar
safety_signals.meaning: player has economic capacity; can continue production and expansion
food production capacity supports unit growth; economy stable

safety_signals.visual_form: dense tree sprites surrounding settlement perimeter
safety_signals.meaning: natural fortification in place; early-game defensive position secure

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Orc units and buildings have distinctly different sprite designs (described as different sprites for functionally equivalent structures)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Human settlements with characteristic architecture; magical buildings include Cleric tower and Conjurer tower

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Orc units and buildings have distinctly different sprite designs from Human counterparts
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Orc settlements with barbaric/dark architectural style; magical buildings include Necrolyte tower and Warlock tower; skull/necromantic visual themes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: forests retained as defensive walls; Town Center and Farms prominent; few military units visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: builder units actively harvesting resources from forests and gold mines; minimal combat activity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Barracks structures visible; multiple unit types present on map; some forest clearance for expansion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: resource gathering continues at accelerated pace; units begin patrol and skirmish missions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: flaming buildings; razed terrain; opposing colored units (red vs blue) visible in proximity; damaged unit sprites
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: units engaged in combat; defensive structures under fire; town under siege conditions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: exhausted mines and forests appear empty/depleted; low gold/lumber numbers in UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: builder units must travel longer distances; economic pressure increases; expansion becomes necessary

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: spellcaster units visible near combat zones; magical building towers prominent in settlement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Clerics/Necrolytes cast protective or summoning spells; summoned elementals or skeletal troops appear on battlefield

environmental_storytelling.example: progression from intact building → flaming building → burnt ground scorch mark tells story of settlement destruction without explicit narration
depleted forests and exhausted gold mines signal passage of time and player pressure; deforestation shows expansion history
distinct sprite sets for identical-function buildings (Human Town Hall vs Orc Town Hall) reinforce cultural/faction identity without UI labels
spellcaster tower locations visually indicate which magical capabilities are available in a settlement
retained forest density around settlements shows defensive preparation level; cleared forests indicate vulnerability

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: visible moving between forest/mine and Town Center; delivers resources that update UI counters
positioned at building site; structure gradually appears and becomes functional
attacking animation; opponent unit displays damage; visual clash on screen
Cleric heals nearby damaged unit (visual repair); Necrolyte raises skeleton troops (new unit appears); Conjurer summons water elemental (summoned unit appears)
sprite transitions to flaming appearance; health implied by visual intensity of flames
replaced by burnt ground scorch mark; no longer occupies space; resource lost
visual presence indicates food supply; unit production restricted until additional farms built (behavioral gate)

notes: Warcraft: Orcs & Humans uses color-coding (blue vs red) as primary faction differentiation combined with distinct sprite work for all structures and units. Danger is communicated through building damage states (flames, char marks) and terrain scarring. Safety is signaled through intact structures, resource availability UI, and defensive forest retention. The game's manifestation layer is minimalist but effective: visual state of buildings communicates mission progression, resource UI numbers communicate economic health, and unit colors immediately identify allegiance. Spellcaster presence modifies both visual and behavioral signals—summoned units, healing animations, and skeletal troops add visual complexity to indicate magical threats. Early-game emphasis on forest retention as defense tells story of resource scarcity and vulnerability without explicit communication.
